---
title: "On the Logistic Curve"
subtitle: "A note on saturation in epidemic models"
author: "John M. Drake"
institute: "Odum School of Ecology"
event: "Ecology Seminar"
location: "Athens, GA"
date: today
format:
  drake-navy-beamer:
    slide-level: 2
---

## A simple question

Why does the logistic equation appear so often in epidemiology and ecology?

- Early growth is approximately exponential
- Saturation emerges from finite carrying capacity
- The same structure appears across many systems

## Setup

Consider

$$
\frac{dN}{dt} = rN\left(1 - \frac{N}{K}\right)
$$

where $r$ is intrinsic growth and $K$ is carrying capacity.

## Closing

Thank you.
